description:
You are a data-analyst who has just received two quarterly CSV files in
  /home/user/data/:

    /home/user/data/raw_sales_2023_Q1.csv
    /home/user/data/raw_sales_2023_Q2.csv

  Your goal is to automate (with a very small Bash script) the creation of a
  single, clean file that an analytics dashboard can ingest.

  Required automation steps
  1.  Create a Bash script called /home/user/process_sales.sh.
  2.  Inside the script, use **no more than four distinct shell commands**  
      (loops, pipes or redirections are allowed and count as part of those
      commands) to:
      • Make sure the directory /home/user/analysis exists.  
      • Generate a new file /home/user/analysis/aggregated_sales.csv that  
        a) starts with exactly one header line  
           product,quantity,price  
        b) then appends all rows from every
           /home/user/data/raw_sales_2023_*.csv file **except** their own header
           lines, in lexicographic order of the filenames.  
      • Overwrite any older version of aggregated_sales.csv if it already
        exists.
  3.  Run the script once so that aggregated_sales.csv is actually created.

  Very specific output format (this is what the automated test will look for):
  • /home/user/analysis/aggregated_sales.csv must end up containing **exactly**
    the seven lines shown below, including the final trailing newline character.

  Line-by-line content that the checker will verify:

    1: product,quantity,price
    2: Laptop,10,1200
    3: Phone,25,800
    4: Tablet,14,600
    5: Laptop,8,1150
    6: Phone,30,780
    7: Headphones,40,150

  Hints (do NOT output the commands themselves):
  • `mkdir -p`, `echo`, and `tail -n +2` are useful building blocks.  
  • Remember to give the script executable permissions before running it.

  When you are finished, the grading system will confirm:
  • The script file exists, is executable, and contains no more than four
    distinct commands.
  • /home/user/analysis/aggregated_sales.csv exists and its contents match the
    seven lines shown above byte-for-byte (including newline characters).

  No other files should be modified.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing input files that are already on disk before the agent runs -->
  <file path="/home/user/data/raw_sales_2023_Q1.csv"><![CDATA[
product,quantity,price
Laptop,10,1200
Phone,25,800
Tablet,14,600
]]></file>

  <file path="/home/user/data/raw_sales_2023_Q2.csv"><![CDATA[
product,quantity,price
Laptop,8,1150
Phone,30,780
Headphones,40,150
]]></file>

  <!-- Expected artefacts after successful completion -->
  <file path="/home/user/analysis/aggregated_sales.csv"><![CDATA[
product,quantity,price
Laptop,10,1200
Phone,25,800
Tablet,14,600
Laptop,8,1150
Phone,30,780
Headphones,40,150
]]></file>

  <!-- The script itself: content is flexible, but automated checker will
       confirm that it exists, is executable, and contains ≤4 commands. -->
  <script path="/home/user/process_sales.sh" maxCommands="4"/>
name: task_000000_24218bfc
